data_type_evidence: claude_code/llm_interface_realistic.py:10-11 — RealisticMockLLM extends MockLLMInterface, a deterministic mock with hardcoded code templates and random.seed(42+seed_offset). No real LLM API calls are made.
claude_code/llm_interface_mock.py:18 — random.seed(42) for reproducibility; all code generation is template-based pattern matching on prompt keywords (lines 28-69).

claude_code/llm_interface_realistic.py:88-94 — Method-specific hardcoded base success rates: 'simple'=0.40, 'reflection'=0.50, 'counterfactual'=0.65. Results are predetermined by these probability differences, not by any actual code generation or learning.
claude_code/llm_interface_realistic.py:16-37 — Task difficulty levels are hardcoded as a dictionary (0.3 to 0.9), determining failure probability per task.
claude_code/llm_interface_realistic.py:71 — Bug introduction is decided by random.random() < failure_prob, making outcomes purely stochastic simulations.
claude_code/tasks.py:6-80 — Programming tasks are simple hardcoded problems (sum of even numbers, palindrome check, etc.) with hardcoded test cases — not real-world benchmarks like HumanEval or MBPP.
claude_code/agents_realistic.py:145-243 — SECACEAgent uses the same mock LLM for counterfactual generation; no actual contrastive alignment training or DPO occurs despite paper claims.
claude_code/run_experiment_final.py:1-3 — File header explicitly states 'Final experiment runner with realistic mock.'
